allowed: The public benchmark management denominator is 300 released rows.
All 300 rows currently have common-row precision evidence for EVAS Python, EVAS Rust, and Spectre AX versus Spectre strict.
EVAS Python, EVAS Rust, and Spectre AX are equivalent to Spectre strict on the full-300 row set under the stated behavior, waveform, edge-window, and task-metric gates.
Task-metric rows may be reported with their metric gate and diagnostic pointwise waveform drift side by side.
forbidden: Do not claim bit-exact waveform equality.
Do not reduce tolerance to one global decimal precision.
Do not treat raw pointwise RMS as the sole acceptance rule for task-metric rows.
Do not claim EVAS is more accurate than Spectre AX.
Do not treat the historical 271-row four-way artifact as the current benchmark denominator.

gates.label: Behavior checker
gates.meaning: Circuit-level function is the primary acceptance signal.
gates.name: behavior_checker

gates.label: Relative waveform RMS
gates.meaning: Pointwise waveform differences are accepted only when normalized RMS error stays inside the gate.
gates.name: relative_waveform
gates.threshold: row_mean<=0.10 and worst_signal<=0.22, or row_mean<=0.08 and worst_signal<=0.25

gates.label: Small absolute voltage
gates.meaning: Rows with small absolute voltage error can pass even when relative error is inflated by tiny signal spans.
gates.name: small_absolute_voltage

gates.label: Edge/discontinuity window
gates.meaning: Solver sampling around discontinuities is not treated as a functional mismatch when the stable region matches.
gates.name: edge_window
gates.threshold: at most 8% of the common sample grid may be excluded when localized to signal activity

gates.label: Task metric
gates.meaning: Some rows are judged by extracted circuit metrics rather than point-by-point waveform equality.
gates.name: task_metric
gates.threshold: checker-owned gain, PLL, lock, frequency, or control tolerance

interpretation.are_they_identical: No. Bit-exact equality is not asserted.
interpretation.how_to_read_tolerance: Tolerance is not one global decimal precision. It is a set of acceptance gates, with Spectre AX/classic self-consistency used as an anchor for waveform drift that exists inside official Spectre modes.
interpretation.row_count_scope: The benchmark management denominator and the current precision evidence row count are both 300. The older 271-row artifact is retained only as historical provenance.
interpretation.task_metric_scope: Rows such as gain extraction measurement flows are accepted by extracted circuit metrics; diagnostic pointwise RMS is shown only to prevent misreading waveform drift as a functional failure.
interpretation.what_differs: Remaining numerical differences are dominated by solver sampling, event timing, edge/discontinuity windows, interpolation, transition/cross behavior, and task-specific metric extraction.
interpretation.what_is_equal: The full 300-row release has common-row precision evidence, and the compared surfaces pass the stated acceptance gates.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: Solver sampling grid
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: solver_sampling_grid
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Use aligned-grid RMS as a diagnostic metric; do not read it as bit-exact equality.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: EVAS and Spectre may save different accepted transient steps, so the report compares common signals on a resampled grid.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: Adaptive solvers choose output points differently even when the circuit-level trajectory is equivalent.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: Event time and cross() localization
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: event_time_and_cross
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Check behavior, event consistency, and edge-window metrics before calling this a mismatch.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: cross() events and timer events can fire at slightly different localized times.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: The two engines use different event scheduling and breakpoint-localization mechanics.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: Edge and discontinuity window
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: edge_window
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Effective metrics may discount a bounded localized window; raw metrics stay visible for audit.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: A few samples around fast edges, discontinuities, or digital thresholds can dominate raw pointwise error.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: One-sample edge placement differences can produce large instantaneous voltage deltas while stable regions match.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: Interpolation on the common grid
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: interpolation
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Treat interpolation error as part of the precision diagnostic, not as the task's functional score.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: Saved waveforms are interpolated before RMS comparison.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: Different native output times require interpolation to compare like with like.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: transition() smoothing
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: transition_smoothing
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Inspect stable-region behavior and checker metrics when transition edges inflate raw RMS.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: transition() ramps can differ slightly in breakpoint placement and sampled slope.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: EVAS and Spectre do not promise identical internal smoothing schedules.

pointwise_difference_taxonomy.label: Task-metric gate
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.name: task_metric_gate
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.reporting_rule: Report the task metric and include pointwise waveform diagnostics only as explanatory context.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.what_changes: Some rows are accepted by extracted circuit metrics, such as gain or lock/frequency values, not by raw pointwise waveform equality.
pointwise_difference_taxonomy.why_expected: Measurement-flow tasks can use dither/noise-like stimulus where pointwise phase is not the design objective.

task_metric_rows.diagnostic_note: Task acceptance uses the extracted circuit metric. The diagnostic waveform-only comparison is retained to explain why raw pointwise RMS is not the score for this row.
